mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening supply chain that enhances productivity and boosts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth and boosts corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts signals a strengthening demand for residential construction that boosts economic growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the Consumer Price Index signals a strengthening inflationary environment that boosts interest rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in real disposable personal income signals a weakening demand for consumer goods that boosts economic growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in weakening demand periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims signals a weakening labor market that boosts wage growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in weakening labor periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain that compresses productivity and boosts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
